items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/` **Triggered by:** commit 5dd1443c4 (PR #58178) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed Post-release version increment of `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` from `1.0.0-beta.1` ‚Üí `1.0.0-beta.2` following the initial package release on 2026-04-14 as part of the major three-package architectural rewrite (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` beta.21). ## Gaps Found - `InvocationContext.InvocationId` is only mentioned in passing in the "Handler lifetime" section and is not described in the **InvocationContext** key concepts subsection ‚Äî important since `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync` both receive an `invocationId` parameter that correlates with this value. - `InvocationContext.QueryParameters` (`IReadOnlyDictionary`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. - `InvocationContext.Isolation` (`IsolationContext`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README ‚Äî leaving multi-tenant scenarios (user/chat isolation keys) completely undocumented. - No manual ASP.NET Core DI setup example ‚Äî the Responses README shows both `ResponsesServer.Run()` and a manual `WebApplication.CreateBuilder()` approach (`AddResponsesServer()` + `MapResponsesServer()`), but the Invocations README shows only `InvocationsServer.Run()`. The equivalent manual setup (`AddInvocationsServer()` + `MapInvocationsServer()`) is missing. - `InvocationsServerOptions` is part of the public API but is never mentioned or described in the README.
üìê Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/README.md` ‚Äî Edit #### 1a. Expand the `InvocationContext` key concept subsection Replace the existing `### InvocationContext` subsection (currently two sentences): ```markdown ### InvocationContext Provides request metadata to the handler, including the resolved session ID and forwarded client headers. ``` with: ```markdown ### InvocationContext Provides request metadata to the handler. All properties are read-only and resolved before `HandleAsync` is called. | Property | Type | Description | |----------|------|-------------| | `InvocationId` | `string` | Unique identifier for this invocation. Passed as the first argument to `GetAsync` and `CancelAsync`. Use as a key when storing per-invocation state. | | `SessionId` | `string` | Resolved multi-turn session identifier (extracted from query parameters or `INVOCATIONS_SESSION_ID` environment variable). | | `ClientHeaders` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | Forwarded `x-client-*` headers from the original request ‚Äî useful for propagating tracing context and client metadata. | | `QueryParameters` | `IReadOnlyDictionary` | All query parameters from the incoming request. | | `Isolation` | `IsolationContext` | Isolation context extracted from `x-agent-user-isolation-key` and `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` headers. Useful for multi-tenant scenarios where per-user or per-chat data must be isolated. `IsolationContext.Empty` indicates no isolation headers were present. | ``` #### 1b. Add a manual DI setup example in `### Configure the server` After the existing Tier 1 snippet block and `EchoHandler` code block (after the closing paragraph about `AgentHostBuilder`), add a new subsection: ```markdown For full control over the ASP.NET Core host, register services manually in your `Program.cs`: ```C# Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer(); builder.Services.AddScoped(); var app = builder.Build(); app.MapInvocationsServer(); app.Run(); ``` ``` #### 1c. Document `InvocationsServerOptions` in the `### Customizing the host` subsection Append after the existing tier sample links: ```markdown To configure `InvocationsServerOptions` (for future options), pass a configure delegate: ```csharp InvocationsServer.Run(configure: builder => { builder.AddInvocations(options => { // InvocationsServerOptions configuration here }); }); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### Snippet: `Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup` **Test file:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs` Add a new test method to the existing `ReadMeSnippets.cs` file: ```csharp [Test] public async Task ManualSetupSnippet() { #region Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(); builder.Services.AddInvocationsServer(); builder.Services.AddScoped(); var app = builder.Build(); app.MapInvocationsServer(); app.Run(); #endregion #if !SNIPPET // Test-only: just verify compilation; don't actually run await Task.CompletedTask; #endif } ``` **README placeholder location:** In the `### Configure the server` subsection of `README.md`, add immediately after the `EchoHandler` class snippet: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:Invocations_ReadMe_ManualSetup ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The existing README already contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ‚úÖ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ‚úÖ Key concepts 3. ‚úÖ Examples 4. ‚úÖ Troubleshooting 5. ‚úÖ Next steps 6. ‚úÖ Contributing Missing/incomplete subsections to update: - `### InvocationContext` ‚Äî update per Step 1a above - `### Configure the server` ‚Äî add manual setup per Step 1b above Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` ‚Äî confirms README is updated with the new snippet 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` ‚Äî only needed if any public types changed (no public API changes expected here) 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [AgentServer] Docs: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations README missing InvocationContext properties and manual DI setup
